column1: Based on the 80-volume Chinese translation by Śikṣānanda [T. 279] and the 40-volume version by Prajñā [T. 293]
Key Turning Points in Sudhana's Journey
Born in Dhanyākara (福城, "City of Blessings") to a wealthy merchant family
First inspired by Mañjuśrī's presence and teachings
Foundation Phase (Teachers 1-10): Basic Buddhist virtues and practices
Expansion Phase (Teachers 11-30): Diverse applications of Dharma
Primary Text: Gaṇḍavyūha Sūtra (T. 279, 80-volume Chinese Avatamsaka)
English Translation: Cleary, T. (1993). The Flower Ornament Scripture (Vol. 3). Shambhala.
Sanskrit Reference: Vaidya, P.L. (1960). Gaṇḍavyūha Sūtra. Darbhanga: Mithila Institute.
